question.text: For determining whether a derivative instrument is an ODI, what is the threshold for non-proprietary indices (e.g., MSCI World)?

answer.text: 30% - trades with Indian underlying less than 30% of the index are not regarded as ODIs
20% - trades with Indian underlying less than 20% of the index are not regarded as ODIs
50% - trades with Indian underlying less than 50% of the index are not regarded as ODIs
10% - trades with Indian underlying less than 10% of the index are not regarded as ODIs

explanation.text: **The Logic:** As per SEBI guidelines, the threshold for non-proprietary indices is 20%, meaning trades where Indian underlying is less than 20% of the index would not be regarded as ODIs. **The Trap:** Custom baskets have no such threshold - they are always treated as ODIs if hedged onshore, regardless of Indian component percentage. **Cross-Exam Context:** This ODI materiality test is specific to NISM Series III-A and is not commonly covered in other NISM exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Non-proprietary index = 20% threshold applies. Custom basket = always an ODI if hedged onshore.
